articles.summary: This study examines the structural characteristics of swine leukocyte antigen (SLA) class I molecules, which are important for understanding immune recognition in swine. articles.title: EGFR-dependent delivery of phosphorylated IGFBP3 by cancer stem cells drives chemotherapy resistance in colorectal cancer
articles.summary: Cancer stem cells in colorectal cancer secrete phosphorylated IGFBP3 protein through an EGFR-dependent mechanism, which promotes chemotherapy resistance in tumor cells. This finding identifies a novel pathway by which cancer stem cells communicate with bulk tumor cells to reduce drug sensitivity. The study suggests that targeting this EGFR-IGFBP3 signaling axis may enhance chemotherapy efficacy in colorectal cancer.

articles.title: Cardiovascular outcomes of empagliflozin-GLP-1RA combination therapy in type 2 diabetes: EMPRISE study
articles.summary: The EMPRISE study evaluated cardiovascular outcomes in type 2 diabetes patients receiving combination therapy with empagliflozin (an SGLT2 inhibitor) and GLP-1 receptor agonists. This pharmacoepidemiological research from Brigham and Women's Hospital examined the safety and efficacy profile of this drug combination in a real-world patient population. The study contributes evidence on whether dual therapy with these two drug classes provides cardiovascular benefits in diabetic patients.

articles.title: Formulation development and optimization of sparfloxacin-loaded solid lipid nanoparticles integrated with microneedles for the treatment of biofilm-infected wounds
articles.summary: Researchers developed and optimized solid lipid nanoparticles containing sparfloxacin, an antibiotic, integrated with microneedles as a delivery system for treating biofilm-infected wounds. This formulation approach combines nanoparticle technology with microneedle delivery to enhance therapeutic efficacy against biofilm infections.

articles.title: Machine Learning-Based PAT Tool for Real-Time Residual Moisture Monitoring in Lyophilized Biopharmaceuticals Using NIR Spectroscopy
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a machine learning-based process analytical technology (PAT) tool that uses near-infrared (NIR) spectroscopy to monitor residual moisture in real-time during lyophilization of biopharmaceutical products. The tool integrates machine learning algorithms with NIR spectroscopic data to enable non-destructive, continuous assessment of moisture content in freeze-dried biopharmaceuticals. This approach supports real-time process control and quality assurance during lyophilization manufacturing.

articles.title: Enhancing Outcome Measurement in Oncology Clinical Trials Through Artificial Intelligence: A Scoping Review
articles.summary: This scoping review examines the application of artificial intelligence to enhance outcome measurement in oncology clinical trials. The review synthesizes evidence on how AI can improve the accuracy, efficiency, and standardization of outcome assessment across oncology trial designs and populations.

articles.title: Calcium signaling pathway implicates a shared genetic basis between psychiatric and cardiovascular diseases
articles.summary: A study identifies calcium signaling pathway involvement in a shared genetic basis between psychiatric and cardiovascular diseases, suggesting common molecular mechanisms underlying both disease categories. This finding, derived from analysis of genetic data, may have implications for understanding comorbidity patterns and identifying potential therapeutic targets relevant to both disease areas.

articles.title: Digital and AI-enabled models in pharmaceutical development and manufacturing: a regulatory-focused industry survey
articles.summary: This industry survey examines the implementation and regulatory considerations of digital and AI-enabled models across pharmaceutical development and manufacturing processes. The study, conducted by experts from academic institutions and industry, provides insights into current practices and regulatory perspectives on integrating these technologies into drug development workflows.

articles.title: Application of plant viruses in biotechnology with relation to artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology
articles.summary: This review examines the application of plant viruses in biotechnology, with emphasis on their integration with artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology approaches. The article explores how these emerging technologies can enhance the development and utilization of plant virus-based biotechnological tools and solutions.

articles.title: Molecular design beyond training data with novel extended objective functionals of generative AI models driven by quantum annealing computer
articles.summary: This study presents a novel approach to molecular design that extends generative AI model capabilities beyond their training data through the use of extended objective functionals combined with quantum annealing computing. The method integrates quantum annealing technology to optimize molecular structure generation, enabling the discovery of compounds with properties not fully represented in the original training datasets. This approach bridges computational chemistry and quantum computing to enhance the exploration of chemical space for drug discovery applications.

articles.title: Proteome-scale quantification of the interactions driving condensate formation of intrinsically disordered proteins
articles.summary: This study quantifies protein-protein interactions at the proteome scale that drive condensate formation by intrinsically disordered proteins, providing systematic characterization of the molecular mechanisms underlying phase separation. The research integrates experimental and computational approaches to identify and measure the specific interactions responsible for condensate assembly across multiple proteins.

articles.title: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) in neuropsychiatric drug discovery: current challenges and future directions
articles.summary: This review examines the application of artificial intelligence in neuropsychiatric drug discovery, identifying current technical and practical obstacles while proposing strategies to advance the field. The authors discuss how AI can address existing limitations in the drug development process for psychiatric medications, though significant challenges remain in implementation and validation. Key considerations for regulatory and development professionals include data quality requirements, model interpretability, and the integration of AI tools within established drug discovery workflows.

articles.summary: This study examines smart, stimuli-responsive metal-organic frameworks designed to deliver antibacterial agents on-demand in the oral cavity. These advanced materials respond to environmental triggers to release therapeutic compounds for treating oral infections. The research represents a novel approach to controlled drug delivery in dental applications.

articles.title: Network-based machine learning to identify biomarkers for systemic lupus erythematosus
articles.summary: Researchers applied network-based machine learning methods to identify biomarkers for systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). This approach integrates computational analysis with protein interaction networks to discover disease-specific molecular signatures. The study leverages collaborative expertise across immunology, bioinformatics, and structural biology to advance SLE diagnosis and understanding.

articles.title: Circulating inflammatory, redox, and apoptosis-related alterations in drug-naive idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis: an exploratory case-control study
articles.summary: This exploratory case-control study examined circulating biomarkers related to inflammation, oxidative stress, and apoptosis in treatment-naive patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis compared to controls. The research identified specific alterations in these three pathological pathways that may contribute to disease pathogenesis in IPF. These findings provide preliminary evidence of the molecular mechanisms underlying idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis that could inform future therapeutic development.

articles.title: Targeted protein degradation: bridging chemical biology and clinical translation
articles.summary: This review examines targeted protein degradation technologies and their translation from chemical biology research into clinical applications, addressing the bridge between laboratory discovery and therapeutic development in this emerging field.

articles.title: Design, synthesis, and biological evaluation of aryl urea derivatives as novel STING inhibitors based on SN-011
articles.summary: Researchers designed and synthesized a series of aryl urea derivatives based on the lead compound SN-011 and evaluated their biological activity as STING inhibitors. The study represents a structure-based medicinal chemistry approach to develop novel inhibitors of the STING pathway. This work was conducted at the State Key Laboratory of Natural Medicines at China Pharmaceutical University.

articles.summary: This retrospective cohort study compared real-world clinical outcomes between darolutamide- and abiraterone-based triplet therapy regimens in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer. The analysis evaluated the effectiveness and safety of these two androgen receptor inhibitor approaches when combined with standard hormone therapy in a clinical practice setting.

articles.title: Antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates: design principles, intracellular delivery, and translational opportunities
articles.summary: This review examines antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates, focusing on their design principles, mechanisms for achieving intracellular delivery, and potential clinical applications. The work addresses key considerations for developing these hybrid therapeutics that combine the targeting specificity of antibodies with the gene-modulating capabilities of oligonucleotides.

articles.title: FLOWR.ROOT - A flow matching-based foundation model for joint multi-purpose structure-aware 3D ligand generation and affinity prediction
articles.summary: FLOWR.ROOT is a flow matching-based foundation model that enables joint multi-purpose 3D ligand generation and binding affinity prediction while incorporating structural information about target proteins. The model, developed by researchers at Pfizer's Machine Learning & Computational Sciences and Computational Chemistry divisions, combines ligand design and affinity forecasting capabilities in a single framework. This integrated approach supports structure-aware drug discovery by generating candidate ligands and predicting their binding characteristics simultaneously.

articles.summary: Researchers developed an in vitro nasal mucosa model using the CI-pNaEC cell line to evaluate ciliation and mucus secretion for preclinical drug testing. This model system enables assessment of key functional characteristics of nasal tissue relevant to pharmaceutical development and safety evaluation.

articles.title: Organoid-AI platforms need integrated governance in drug discovery
articles.summary: Organoid-AI platforms are functioning as integrated decision systems in drug discovery, influencing compound prioritization, toxicity assessment, and program progression. Current governance approaches validate the biological and computational components separately, despite their interdependent roles in these systems. Integrated governance frameworks are needed to address the combined decision-making functions of organoid-AI platforms in drug development.

articles.summary: Two open-label clinical trials demonstrate that Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) immunotherapy modulates central nervous system immunity and produces measurable changes in Alzheimer's disease biomarkers. These findings suggest BCG may alter the immune environment in the brain through a reprogramming mechanism relevant to neurodegenerative disease pathology.

articles.summary: Ferrocene is an organometallic compound discovered in 1951 that continues to be of interest in medicinal chemistry due to its exceptional stability and has emerging applications as a basis for drug molecules. This comprehensive review examines the chemistry, pharmacology, and therapeutic potential of ferrocene-based drug molecules in medicinal chemistry.

articles.title: Gabapentin Extended Release Tablets in Healthy Subjects Under Fed Conditions: A Randomized, Open-Label, Two-Treatment, Two-Period, Two-Sequence, Single Dose, Crossover, Comparative Bioavailability Study
articles.summary: This randomized, open-label, crossover bioavailability study compared gabapentin extended-release tablets in healthy subjects under fed conditions using a two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence design with single-dose administration. The study was conducted by Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited and collaborating institutions to establish comparative bioavailability data for the formulation. Published in Drugs R&D, the research provides pharmacokinetic characterization of gabapentin extended-release tablets relevant to regulatory assessment and formulation development.

articles.title: Cytotoxicity and synergistic anticancer activity of Kigelia africana (Lam.) Benth. and Spathodea campanulata P. Beauv. polyherbal extracts
articles.summary: This study evaluated the cytotoxic and anticancer properties of polyherbal extracts combining Kigelia africana and Spathodea campanulata, investigating their individual and synergistic effects against cancer cell lines. The research demonstrates that these plant extracts exhibit cytotoxic activity and produce enhanced anticancer effects when used in combination. Findings suggest potential therapeutic applications of these polyherbal formulations in cancer treatment.
